summary: Unmerged/superseded decomp PR for sysdolphin baselib hsd_3AA7. It added large partial implementations around the CARD/save-file command path, fully matched fn_803AC3F8, and improved fn_803ADF90, fn_803AE7F8, and fn_803B0E9C according to the decomp.dev bot. It also refined hsd_3AA7.h prototypes and struct typing, plus made mostly formatting-only changes in psdisp.c and minor cast-spacing style fixes in gm/gr files. The PR was closed after the author noted it was superseded by PR #2512, so future reuse should verify against the successor PR before copying details.

key_files.role: Main decomp work: added HsdCmdEntry/CardStateExt helpers, included hsd_3B2B.h, matched fn_803AC3F8, and added large partial bodies for fn_803ADF90, fn_803AE7F8, and fn_803B0E9C.

Header cleanup: replaced UNK prototypes for hsd_803AAA48 and fn_803AD16C, gave typed state/data/async/callback signatures to several hsd_3AA7 functions, and exposed a more detailed hsd_803AC3E0_arg0_t layout.
Mostly formatting/readability changes in psDispParticles and include ordering; no clear matching impact is shown in the excerpt.
Style-only spacing change for GXColor compound literals.
Style-only spacing change for function pointer casts.
Style-only spacing change for a function pointer cast.

what_changed: Added hsd_3B2B.h include in hsd_3AA7.c, likely to use hsd_803B2B20 for digest/checksum work in fn_803B0E9C.
Changed CardQueueEntry.x4 from s32 to void*, then cast it back where fn_803AA790 needs an integer command argument.
Introduced HsdCmdEntry and CardStateExt local structs, including CARDFileInfo, block maps, data buffers, and fields such as x170/x270/x370/x3B0/x460.
Added CMD_QUEUE(base) macro for treating an offset from a base pointer as an HsdCmdEntry array.
Reworked fn_803AC3F8 from a partially matched version with manual remaining/unroll cleanup into a concise 100% match that writes up to three file descriptor entries and zero-fills the remainder.

Removed placeholder markers for fn_803ADF90, fn_803AE7F8, and fn_803B0E9C and replaced them with large partial implementations covering block mapping, sequence checks, CARDRead/CARDWrite/CARDFastOpen/CARDClose retry loops, async command submission through fn_803AC168, memcpy/memset payload handling, and digest generation.
Adjusted fn_803B1F78 and fn_803B21E8 call sites/signatures to pass typed hsd_803AC3E0_arg0_t/CardState-related pointers rather than raw s32 card_state values.

Updated hsd_3AA7.h to make hsd_803AAA48 return s32 with no args, fn_803AD16C take CardState*, and several file operations take struct hsd_803AC3E0_arg0_t* plus file_id/data/async/callback-style arguments.
Applied formatting-only spacing changes to GXColor compound literals and function pointer casts in gm_18A5.c, gmclassic.c, and gricemt.c.
Reformatted many long lines in psdisp.c, including matrix/projection calculations, GXSetArray/GXSetVtxAttrFmt/GXSetBlendMode/GXSetAlphaCompare/GXSetChanCtrl calls, and color arithmetic.
smart_moves: Using a local CardStateExt overlay let the author name and index the save/card state fields needed by several functions without prematurely committing to a globally perfect public struct.
The fn_803AC3F8 fix simplified zero-fill control flow instead of preserving an artificial unrolled cleanup path, which the bot confirmed as a full match.
Typed header prototypes made call sites express state pointer/data/async/callback roles, reducing raw s32 ambiguity in later hsd_3AA7 work.
The implementations consistently model CARD busy retry behavior with bounded retry loops around CARDRead, CARDWrite, CARDFastOpen, and CARDClose.
The async path is kept structurally distinct from the sync CARD API path by building 9-word command buffers and routing through fn_803AC168.
The block-repair/read logic records physical/logical block maps and sequence numbers separately, which is useful for future work on this save-file subsystem.
decomp_lessons: For CARD/save decomp, separate physical block number, logical file block index, sequence number, and file_id variables; this made fn_803ADF90 and fn_803AE7F8 easier to reason about than raw argN-only code.
A partial overlay struct can unlock multiple functions in a subsystem, but future work should validate offsets before promoting it to a public header.
When a function writes fixed-size table entries, a simple counted loop plus explicit zero-fill can match better than hand-preserved unrolled code from an earlier guess.
Header prototypes should reflect pointer identity as soon as it is known; changing raw s32 parameters to struct hsd_803AC3E0_arg0_t* clarified fn_803ADF90/fn_803AE7F8/fn_803B21E8 usage.

Keep @todo comments for known nonmatching causes, such as volatile load scheduling, rlwimi emission, and stwx-vs-stw+disp rollback differences, because they preserve matching context for the next pass.
Because this PR was superseded and unmerged, treat it as a research snapshot rather than canonical project history; compare against PR #2512 before reusing implementation details.
naming_conventions: Local overlay structs used CamelCase names such as HsdCmdEntry, Hsd803B2550Ctx, and CardStateExt.
Unknown fields remained offset-style names such as x0, x4C, x170, x270, x370, x3B0, and x460.
Function parameters were improved from generic idx to file_idx where the role was known.
Header prototypes used semantic names like state, file_id, data, async, and callback for newly typed hsd_3AA7 operations.
Function pointer cast formatting followed the style `(u8 (*)(s32, s32, u8))` and `(s32 (*)(HSD_GObj*, s32*))` with a space before `(*)`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: fn_803AC3F8 reached 100% by changing the descriptor loop expression, using `file_idx`, casting x4C to u32 for shifts, preserving the unusual comma-expression shift `>> ((0, 0x10))`, and replacing a manual unroll cleanup with a `for (; i < 3; i++)` zero-fill.

Existing todo notes identify remaining matching blockers: fn_803AC168 has volatile load scheduling issues in a critical section, fn_803AC6B8 has an mwcc `rlwimi. r6,r0,8,10,23` emission issue, and fn_803ADE4C/fn_803B1F78 have stwx versus stw+disp rollback differences.
Async command submission used stack arrays of nine s32 words with command IDs such as 2, 4, 5, 6, 8, 9, and 10, then called fn_803AC168.
Synchronous CARD access modeled hardware/API behavior with retry loops that break when the CARD result is not -1 and cap retries at 10.
The fn_803B0E9C partial used hsd_803B2B20 digest generation over sector-sized chunks and appended digest bytes before CARDWrite.

review_feedback.feedback: Matched code increased to 67.19% (+0.01%, +352 bytes). One new match: hsd_3AA7 fn_803AC3F8 from 82.55% to 100.00%.
Reported three unmatched improvements: fn_803AE7F8 from 0.00% to 42.42%, fn_803ADF90 from 0.00% to 56.38%, and fn_803B0E9C from 0.00% to 49.05%.
Reported one regression: fn_803B21E8 from 94.85% to 92.73% (-8 bytes).
PR was closed as superseded by https://github.com/doldecomp/melee/pull/2512.
No human review comments or reviews were present in the supplied slice.

follow_up_queries: Compare PR #2478 against superseding PR #2512 to identify which hsd_3AA7 implementations and struct fields were kept, renamed, or discarded.
Search for later changes to CardStateExt or hsd_803AC3E0_arg0_t to determine the canonical struct layout for the hsd_3AA7 CARD/save subsystem.
Check whether fn_803ADF90, fn_803AE7F8, and fn_803B0E9C eventually matched and which matching tactics resolved the remaining percentages.
Investigate the fn_803B21E8 regression noted by decomp.dev and whether typed card_state access was later adjusted.
Review future PRs touching fn_803AC168 for the volatile load scheduling issue documented in the todo comment.
